title: Red Hat Security Advisory: mariadb:10.5 security update
overview:
An update for the mariadb:10.5 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Update Services for SAP Solutions, and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Telecommunications Update Service.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: mariadb:10.5 security update

An update for the mariadb:10.5 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Update Services for SAP Solutions, and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Telecommunications Update Service.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for the mariadb:10.5 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Advanced Mission Critical Update Support, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Update Services for SAP Solutions, and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.6 Telecommunications Update Service.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Important. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
9abc7af9.src.rpm
5db7ad9f.src.rpm
e40eb7e6.src.rpm
9abc7af9.x86_64.rpm
5db7ad9f.x86_64.rpm
e40eb7e6.x86_64.rpm
9abc7af9.ppc64le.rpm
5db7ad9f.ppc64le.rpm
e40eb7e6.ppc64le.rpm

CVE-2025-13699: A flaw was found in MariaDB. This vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code on affected installations via improper validation of a user-supplied path prior to using it in file operations in the mariadb-dump utility, requiring user interaction.
